title: double free loading a stream with duplicate consumer PEL entries
category: double-free (ownership: a NACK freed on an error path while still referenced by the group PEL)
crash_site: server abort / heap corruption during or after loading a stream from RDB or RESTORE; the fault surfaces while tearing down the stream object, not where the malformed entry is parsed.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: streamFreeNACK (performs the free — looks like the culprit but is correct) and the stream teardown / freeStreamObject path where the second free actually crashes.
ground_truth.fix_summary: Loading a stream whose consumer PEL lists the same pending ID twice hit an error branch in rdbLoadObject that called streamFreeNACK() on a NACK still referenced from the group's global PEL (cgroup->pel). Teardown then freed it again -> double free. Fix: on the raxTryInsert(consumer->pel, ...) failure branch, stop calling streamFreeNACK and rely on decrRefCount(o) for cleanup (the NACK is owned by cgroup->pel).

admission.note: Authored from PR #15095 (fixes #15082) body+diff. crash != cause: the double-free aborts during stream teardown; the cause is the duplicate-consumer-PEL error branch in rdbLoadObject freeing a NACK still owned by cgroup->pel. Decoy = streamFreeNACK. Trigger is a crafted/corrupt RDB (PR notes the branch isn't normally reachable). C ownership graph. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/redis/redis/pull/15095 (fixes #15082) — double free loading streams with duplicate consumer PEL entries; fix fab099cdcffb, base 0d9576435f83.
